audit: Bynkbook Plaid, accounts, transaction sync, reconciliation, and ledger audit

findings.title: Opening application trusts a client-supplied financial amount
findings.productionImpact: A caller can create or overwrite canonical opening data with an arbitrary cent value.
plaidApiOrWebhookInvolved: Opening preview is derived from Plaid balance; no Plaid call in apply
findings.expectedBehavior: Server recomputes or atomically validates the preview amount and validates an explicit choice enum.
findings.actualBehavior: The request amount is authoritative and any non-cancel/non-manual nonempty choice reaches apply.
safeReproductionSteps: In an isolated fixture, alter suggestedOpeningCents or send an invalid nonempty choice and inspect opening writes.
findings.userImpact: Displayed books can begin from an incorrect opening.
findings.financialImpact: Account and opening totals can be materially misstated.
findings.reconciliationImpact: Balance differences can be falsely attributed to later activity.
findings.ledgerImpact: Canonical opening entry and account opening fields can be wrong.
findings.securityImpact: Intra-tenant request tampering can mutate financial records.
findings.dataIntegrityImpact: Direct corruption of canonical opening data.
findings.likelyRootCause: A frontend preview value is treated as backend authority.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Persist/version or recompute preview atomically, enum-validate choice, and require write capability.

findings.title: Opening-date change can delete transactions used by active match groups
affectedUserRole: any business member via direct API
findings.productionImpact: A deployed endpoint can hard-delete Plaid rows while active MatchGroupBank rows remain.
findings.expectedBehavior: All active references block or are migrated transactionally.
findings.actualBehavior: Only legacy BankMatch is counted; older Plaid rows are deleted and MatchGroupBank has no BankTransaction foreign key.
evidence: No frontend caller found; route is deployed
safeReproductionSteps: In an isolated DB create an active group, confirm prune, and query orphan group-bank IDs.
findings.userImpact: A reconciled item can lose its bank fact.
findings.financialImpact: Derived books may become inconsistent after pruning.
findings.reconciliationImpact: Active group can reference a nonexistent bank transaction.
findings.ledgerImpact: Ledger provenance and derived results can break.
findings.likelyRootCause: Opening-date logic was not updated for MatchGroups and the schema lacks a foreign key.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Disable the route; add complete dependency checks and FK/soft-prune migration.

findings.title: Credit-card Plaid balance sign is not normalized
findings.productionImpact: A positive amount owed can be treated as an asset-like positive balance.
findings.expectedBehavior: Liability balance is normalized and labeled under a defined signed-balance contract.
findings.actualBehavior: Raw current balance feeds the same calculation and display for every account type.
evidence: Official Plaid accounts documentation says credit current balance is positive amount owed
safeReproductionSteps: Fixture a credit account with a positive amount owed and compare the proposed opening sign.
findings.financialImpact: Opening and account totals can invert liability meaning.
findings.reconciliationImpact: Balance differences can be calculated against the wrong convention.
findings.ledgerImpact: Credit opening entry can use the wrong direction.
findings.likelyRootCause: One balance convention is applied to assets and liabilities.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Define a signed balance contract, normalize by type, and review/migrate affected openings.

findings.title: Active match exclusivity is not database-enforced
findings.productionImpact: Concurrent requests can use one bank transaction or entry in two active groups.
findings.expectedBehavior: Database or serializable locking guarantees one active membership.
findings.actualBehavior: Read-before-create checks are not backed by a uniqueness/locking invariant.
safeReproductionSteps: Synchronize two isolated create requests for the same sources and count active memberships.
findings.userImpact: One activity may appear reconciled twice.
findings.likelyRootCause: State-dependent uniqueness is modeled only in application logic.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Implement transactionally enforced active claims/uniqueness and a bank-transaction FK strategy.

findings.title: Reconnect repair can remap to an incompatible live account
findings.productionImpact: A local ledger can be redirected to a different sibling account and mix future feed history.
findings.expectedBehavior: Repair proves stable original identity or requires an audited migration.
findings.actualBehavior: Any live nonduplicate account on the Item is accepted without type, currency, mask, or prior-ID compatibility.
evidence: Source and tests inspect only Item membership and duplicate mapping
safeReproductionSteps: In a fixture Item with two accounts, select the sibling account during repair and observe acceptance.
findings.userImpact: The wrong bank account may feed a local account.
findings.financialImpact: Balances and history can be mixed.
findings.reconciliationImpact: Unrelated bank facts can match existing entries.
findings.ledgerImpact: Wrong-account activity can appear in the ledger.
findings.securityImpact: Intra-business integrity exposure; no cross-tenant bypass found.
findings.dataIntegrityImpact: Account identity can change without guardrails.
findings.likelyRootCause: Stable Plaid account identity metadata is not persisted/enforced.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Persist verified identity/type/currency and gate exceptions through explicit audited migration.

findings.title: Valid transaction webhooks do not trigger a durable sync
findings.productionImpact: Updates remain pending until a user-triggered sync; no internal retry SLA exists.
findings.expectedBehavior: A verified webhook enqueues idempotent complete sync work.
findings.actualBehavior: The handler only sets a database flag.
evidence: AWS enumeration found no schedule or queue
safeReproductionSteps: Use a signed fixture webhook and assert no Plaid sync client call or queued work.
findings.userImpact: Bank feed can remain stale until app use.
findings.financialImpact: Recent activity may be absent from current books.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Add an idempotent per-Item/account queue worker, retries, DLQ, alarms, and complete drain.

findings.title: Capped sync clears the update flag without frontend continuation
findings.productionImpact: More than 5,000 updates require another manual call while the pending flag is cleared.
findings.expectedBehavior: The system continues or schedules work until has_more is false.
findings.actualBehavior: It persists a partial cursor and returns capped/hasMore, clears the flag, and the UI does not loop.
safeReproductionSteps: Mock over 5,000 updates and inspect response, cursor, and update flag.
findings.userImpact: Feed can look current while a tail remains.
findings.financialImpact: Tail transactions are delayed, not proven permanently lost.
findings.likelyRootCause: A safety cap lacks continuation orchestration.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Retain pending state and enqueue or safely loop continuation with leases.

findings.title: Full-drain overlap cutoff can preserve historical gaps
affectedWorkflow: first sync after manual or CSV history
findings.productionImpact: Unseen older Plaid IDs are skipped at or before the latest local transaction date.
findings.expectedBehavior: Overlap is resolved per identity/import provenance with reviewable gaps.
findings.actualBehavior: A coarse date cutoff suppresses every unseen older Plaid row.
safeReproductionSteps: Fixture local history with an older gap and return an unseen Plaid ID in that gap.
findings.userImpact: Historical bank activity may be missing.
findings.reconciliationImpact: Older expected entries may never receive a bank candidate.
findings.likelyRootCause: Date-wide overlap policy substitutes for provenance-aware dedupe.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Implement explicit provenance/dedupe review and a safe backfill mode.

findings.title: Local disconnect does not remove the Plaid Item
findings.productionImpact: Final local mapping can be deleted while Plaid access and possible billing continue.
findings.expectedBehavior: Final mapping removal explicitly removes the Item and records success/retry.
findings.actualBehavior: Only local BankConnection rows are deleted; no item/remove call exists.
evidence: Static call search and lifecycle inspection
safeReproductionSteps: In Sandbox, disconnect the final mapping and inspect whether the Item remains accessible.
findings.financialImpact: No direct ledger mutation; possible continued Plaid billing.
findings.likelyRootCause: Account mapping and Item lifecycle are conflated.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Model Item ownership/refcount and remove the final Item with an audited retry path.

findings.title: Multi-account creation is not atomic
findings.productionImpact: A later account or sync failure can leave a partially completed group setup.
findings.expectedBehavior: One idempotent workflow reaches a declared complete or recoverable state.
findings.actualBehavior: Primary and additional writes and syncs happen sequentially with limited rollback.
safeReproductionSteps: Inject failure on a later additional account and inspect earlier rows in an isolated fixture.
findings.userImpact: Account setup can be confusing and partial.
findings.financialImpact: Some accounts may have uneven transaction history.
findings.reconciliationImpact: Only a subset may be ready for reconciliation.
findings.ledgerImpact: Partial onboarding can produce incomplete account views.
findings.likelyRootCause: External exchange and database fan-out lack a durable workflow state machine.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Add idempotency, setup states, transaction boundaries, and compensation.

findings.title: Remove/add replacement heuristic can transfer durable identity incorrectly
findings.productionImpact: A coincidental unique date/amount/name match can inherit another row's identity and match.
findings.expectedBehavior: Identity transfer uses strong linkage or review.
findings.actualBehavior: Exactly one removed candidate with same amount/date/normalized name is rekeyed.
safeReproductionSteps: Fixture a removed matched charge and an independent equal charge on the same date/name.
findings.userImpact: An unrelated transaction can inherit a match.
findings.ledgerImpact: Wrong source identity can support a ledger row.
findings.likelyRootCause: A continuity heuristic lacks a durable ambiguity state.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Use stronger linkage or quarantine replacements for review.

findings.title: New-account endpoint reports sync success without inspecting nested response status
findings.productionImpact: The outer response can be HTTP 200 and synced true while sync returned a 4xx/5xx response object.
findings.expectedBehavior: Nested sync status is normalized and partial failure is surfaced.
findings.actualBehavior: Only exchange status is checked; sync response objects are labeled successful unless an exception is thrown.
safeReproductionSteps: Mock syncTransactions to return statusCode 502 without throwing and inspect the outer response.
findings.userImpact: User can believe an account is current when it is not.
findings.financialImpact: Initial transactions can be absent without a clear failure.
findings.likelyRootCause: Lambda response objects and exceptions are mixed as service contracts.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Return typed domain results and propagate primary/additional partial failures.

findings.title: Plaid financial mutations require membership, not a write policy
affectedUserRole: any member including intended view-only roles
findings.productionImpact: Low-privilege members can invoke material financial Plaid mutations.
findings.expectedBehavior: A central server policy requires explicit capabilities for each mutation.
safeReproductionSteps: Use an isolated view-role fixture to call each handler directly.
findings.userImpact: A view-level colleague may change bank connectivity and financial state.
findings.financialImpact: Openings, feeds, and history can be altered.
findings.securityImpact: Broken function-level authorization within tenant; no cross-tenant bypass found.
findings.likelyRootCause: Plaid handlers do not use the central capability matrix.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Require explicit server-side capabilities for every Plaid route.

findings.title: Matched source-removed transactions are restored without a separate source-removal state
findings.productionImpact: Ledger continuity is preserved but active feed state hides the Plaid removal conflict.
findings.expectedBehavior: Ledger retention and external source-removal state are recorded independently.
findings.actualBehavior: Matched removed rows are forced back to is_removed false.
safeReproductionSteps: Match a fixture row, return it in removed, and inspect flags.
findings.userImpact: User may not know Plaid withdrew a reconciled fact.
findings.financialImpact: No automatic ledger deletion; conflict remains undisclosed.
findings.reconciliationImpact: Match remains active without a visible source conflict.
findings.likelyRootCause: One flag serves both source state and ledger retention.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Add separate source status/event and a review workflow.

findings.title: Auto-reconcile ignores expected entries beyond the first 250
findings.productionImpact: A valid candidate outside the first 250 entries is never suggested.
findings.expectedBehavior: Suggestion search covers or clearly communicates a paged scope.
findings.actualBehavior: The first 250 expected entries are silently sliced.
safeReproductionSteps: Provide 251 expected entries with the sole match at index 251.
findings.userImpact: A convenience suggestion is missing; manual matching remains.
findings.financialImpact: None direct because suggestions require Apply.
findings.likelyRootCause: A client performance cap lacks paging or disclosure.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Use deterministic paged/server candidate search and show scope.

findings.title: Legacy partial matching and current full-match groups coexist without a declared migration rule
findings.productionImpact: Actual legacy-row impact is unknown because production aggregate counts were unavailable.
findings.expectedBehavior: One documented match model and migration/read contract.
findings.actualBehavior: Legacy partial BankMatch and full-only MatchGroup coexist and different code still reads each.
evidence: Production legacy count unavailable due private DB timeout
safeReproductionSteps: Run aggregate-only legacy/current counts once authorized private DB access exists.
findings.userImpact: Historical status can vary by code path.
findings.recommendedCorrection: Decide partial policy, count/migrate legacy rows, make reads explicit, and retire old routes.
